name: eventbus
description: A concurrency-safe, in-process topic-based publish/subscribe event bus built on channels and sync. Subscribe(topic) returns a receive-only <-chan Event, Publish(topic, event) fans the event out non-blockingly to every current subscriber of that topic, and Close() closes all subscriber channels and makes the bus idempotently inert. Standard library only, go 1.23, gofmt-clean. Verified GREEN: go build, go vet, go test, and go test -race all pass.

maintain.request: Add an Unsubscribe(topic string, ch <-chan Event) bool method so a single subscriber can cancel its subscription without tearing down the whole bus. It must remove that one channel from the topic, close it so any range loop draining it terminates, leave the other subscribers on that topic intact, and report whether a matching subscription was actually found (returning false for unknown channels or on an already-closed bus).

review.bug: Publish reads the shared subs map and sends on subscriber channels without acquiring b.mu (the RLock/RUnlock pair was dropped), creating a data race with Subscribe and Close that can corrupt the map and panic by sending on a channel Close is concurrently closing.
keywords: publish does not hold the lock
